commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh You have been in Eden the garden of God; every precious stone was your covering, the sardius, topaz, and the diamond, the beryl, the onyx, and the jasper, the sapphire, the emerald, and the carbuncle, and gold: the workmanship of your timbrels and of your pipes was prepared in you in the day that you were created. Thou hadst every thing delightful at Tyre. (Calmet) Covering. Hebrew, "bandage "diadem, (Haydock) or belt. Septuagint specify twelve stones. See Exodus xxviii. Pipes; music used at the coronation, which was celebrated as a birth-day. (Calmet) God gives and withdraws power from all kings. As long as they act well, they are in a sort of sanctuary.
